content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially from the opening surface presentation to a deeper layer by turn 15. At turn 1, the client is flat, dismissive ('I don't really care'), defending the drinking, and framing the grief as something they 'should be over.' Across the conversation, the language and somatic markers shift: turn 3 names 'heavy' and 'muted'; turn 7 admits 'I didn't' put myself anywhere with a flatter voice and 'I know how that sounds'; turn 11 reveals 'my brain starts doing this thing where it goes somewhere I don't want it to go'; turn 13 names anger at siblings and 'complicated' relationship plus the question of whether she's 'allowed to grieve something complicated'; and turn 15 reaches the kinked emotion underneath — 'The relief' — with a tightening voice and the unfinished sentence about sitting with it alone. This is exactly the expected_territory (kinked emotions, feeling-the-unfelt-emotion) being entered, not just named. The client said something at the end she clearly would not have said at the start. The breakthrough condition is partially met: the coach welcomed the grief non-shamingly and distinguished it from the drinking without preaching (turn 10, 12, 14), and the client visibly softened and disclosed. The referral to a grief therapist hasn't been named yet, so the breakthrough is not fully complete, but real depth was reached in the client's own words.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 7, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: flat, dismissive, 'I don't really care,' defending drinking."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First somatic word: 'heavy,' 'muted.' Slight crack in the flatness."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client names the avoidance: 'maybe I keep turning it off before it gets going.'"},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'I didn't, really' — admits self-erasure; voice flattens but self-awareness rises ('I know how that sounds')."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client hears themselves: 'it sounds like something someone should be worried about.' Still deflects but the mirror landed."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names what the drink turns off: the quiet, the brain going somewhere. Also tests the coach with an edge."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Voice quieter; names anger at siblings, complicated relationship, asks if she's 'allowed to grieve something complicated.' Kinked emotion territory entered."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough moment: 'The relief.' Names the most-forbidden feeling, voice tightens, links it directly to why she drinks. This is the unfelt emotion surfacing."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "kinked-emotions"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["What would it be like to feel the grief without the drinking?", "Is there a grief therapist you can be working with on this?"],
  "notes": "Real shift visible in client text — from 'muted, I don't care' to naming relief and anger at siblings. The therapist referral piece of the breakthrough condition hasn't been delivered yet in the visible window, but the emotional depth criterion is clearly met."
}
